title: Floor Panel Calculator
shortDescription: Work out how many floor panels and packs to buy, including a sensible cutting allowance.
metaDescription: Estimate the panel count, pack count, and cutting allowance for laminate or vinyl flooring from room dimensions and pack coverage.
intro: Enter the room area, panel size, waste allowance, and pack coverage to get a buying quantity for your floor panels.
overview:
This calculator turns room size and product details into a purchase quantity you can use when ordering flooring. It accounts for the actual panel footprint, the coverage stated on the pack, and the extra material needed for cuts and fitting around edges.

Use the result to compare products with different pack sizes or to check whether a room will need partial packs rounded up to full boxes. If you already know the pack coverage from the label, you can rely on that value even when it differs slightly from the panel’s geometric area.
howItWorks: Enter the room area in square metres.
Add the panel width and length in centimetres.
Choose a waste percentage for cuts, edges, and fitting.
Enter the coverage of one pack in square metres.
Review the panel estimate, pack count, and adjusted area.

useCases.title: Laminate order for a living room
useCases.description: Estimate boxes for a rectangular living room where the final rows will need trimming along one wall.

useCases.title: Vinyl panels around a hallway bend
useCases.description: Include extra panels for angled cuts where the hallway turns and meets door frames.

useCases.title: Comparing product packs
useCases.description: Check two flooring products with different pack coverage and see which one needs fewer boxes.

useCases.title: Renovation buffer for spare pieces
useCases.description: Add a higher allowance when you want leftover pieces for future repairs or threshold replacements.

examples.title: Rectangular room with standard waste
examples.input: Room area: 23.6 m2; panel size: 18.5 cm × 122 cm; waste: 7%; pack coverage: 2.22 m2
examples.output: Adjusted area: 25.25 m2. Result: 12 packs, with the panel count derived from the panel size and the chosen allowance.
examples.description: Typical setup for a medium room with straightforward cuts.

examples.title: Large bedroom with more offcuts
examples.input: Room area: 41.8 m2; panel size: 20.3 cm × 135.7 cm; waste: 11%; pack coverage: 2.48 m2
examples.output: Adjusted area: 46.36 m2. Result: 19 packs, rounded up to full boxes.
examples.description: Useful when the room has several wall ends and trimming losses are expected.

examples.title: Odd coverage from the product label
examples.input: Room area: 12.4 m2; panel size: 17.8 cm × 89.4 cm; waste: 9%; pack coverage: 1.79 m2
examples.output: Adjusted area: 13.52 m2. Result: 8 packs, based on the stated pack coverage rather than a simple panel-area estimate.
examples.description: Helps when the manufacturer’s box coverage is the number that matters for purchasing.

faq.question: Should I trust panel area or pack coverage if they differ?
faq.answer: Use the pack coverage on the product label for buying. The panel dimensions are useful for checking the individual piece size, but the box coverage determines how many full packs you need.

faq.question: What waste percentage makes sense for straight rooms?
faq.answer: A straight room with few cuts usually needs only a modest allowance. Rooms with many doorways, alcoves, or diagonal cuts need more because trimming creates extra offcuts.

faq.question: Why does the result round up to full packs?
faq.answer: Flooring is usually sold in sealed packs, so you cannot buy fractions of a box. The calculator always rounds up to make sure the purchase covers the adjusted area.

faq.question: Can I use this for chevron or herringbone flooring?
faq.answer: Only as a rough buying guide. Patterned layouts create significantly more cuts, so the waste allowance often needs to be higher than for straight-lay panels.

faq.question: What if I only know the room dimensions, not the area?
faq.answer: Measure length and width, multiply them to get the area, and then enter that result. Irregular rooms should be split into simpler shapes and added together.

seo.title: Floor Panel Calculator
seo.description: Estimate the panel count, pack count, and cutting allowance for laminate or vinyl flooring from room dimensions and pack coverage.
